name: Thích Quảng Đức
cause: Persecution of Buddhists by the Catholic Ngô Đình Diệm regime
aftermath: Intensified international pressure on Diệm regime. Contributed to the coup that overthrew Diệm five months later. The photograph became one of the most iconic images of the 20th century.

name: Jan Palach
cause: Protest against Soviet occupation and public apathy following the invasion
broader_context: Warsaw Pact invasion of Czechoslovakia, suppression of Prague Spring reforms
aftermath: Sparked widespread mourning and protests. Became a symbol of Czech resistance. His grave became a pilgrimage site. Inspired further self-immolations by Jan Zajíc and Evžen Plocek in the following weeks.

name: Mohamed Bouazizi
cause: Humiliation and harassment by municipal officials, confiscation of his cart, lack of economic opportunity
broader_context: Corruption, unemployment, and authoritarian rule in Ben Ali's Tunisia
aftermath: Triggered the Tunisian Revolution, which overthrew Ben Ali within a month. Widely credited as the spark that ignited the Arab Spring across the Middle East and North Africa.

name: Aaron Bushnell
cause: Protest against U.S. complicity in the Israeli military campaign in Gaza
broader_context: Israel-Gaza war following October 7, 2023 attack, U.S. military aid to Israel
target: U.S. government, U.S. military support for Israel
aftermath: Reignited debate over U.S. involvement in the Gaza conflict. Became a symbol within the pro-Palestinian movement. Prompted discussions about moral injury within the U.S. military.

name: Mohammad Asif Javed Jutt
cause: Nine-year failed legal battle against Nestlé after being fired for attempting to form a union
broader_context: Labor rights suppression by multinational corporations in Pakistan, inaccessible justice system for working class
aftermath: Left-wing parties and trade unions formed an alliance to seek justice and support his family. Sparked renewed debate about labor rights and corporate impunity in Pakistan.
